originalTitle: Strike over high fuel prices paralyses transport in Kenya
title: Strike over high fuel prices paralyses transport in Kenya
content: Key roads have remained largely empty and some schools have asked students to stay at home.

originalTitle: From escaping child marriage 'to an old pervert' to becoming Sierra Leone's first lady
title: Meet Sierra Leone's Kind First Lady: A Story of Dreams!
content:
Hello, my little explorers and dreamers! I'm your kid-friendly news reporter, here to tell you an amazing story from our beautiful continent, Africa! Today, we are zooming into a country called Sierra Leone. It's a land of sunny beaches, green forests, and wonderful people. Guess what? We are going to meet a very special lady. Her name is Yvonne. She is the First Lady of Sierra Leone! Do you know what a First Lady is? It's like being the main helper for all the people in her country, especially for children and families. She works with the President, who is like the boss of the country, to make life better for everyone.

Yvonne's Brave Start

When Yvonne was a little girl, like some of you, she had a difficult start. Sometimes, grown-up life can be tricky, and children can face big challenges. But Yvonne was very, very brave. More than anything, she wanted to go to school and learn! Learning was her super power, and she knew it would help her fly high. Did you know? Sierra Leone is in West Africa! It's famous for its beautiful diamond mines and its friendly people. She made brave choices to follow her dream of learning. She wanted to fill her mind with books and ideas. It wasn't always easy, but Yvonne had a strong heart and a determined spirit. She always believed that if she worked hard, she could make her dreams come true.

School is Super!

School was Yvonne's favorite place. She loved to read, write, and learn new things every single day. She understood that going to school was like building a strong bridge to her future. Each lesson was a new step on that bridge. Did you know? Education is super important! When kids go to school, they learn to read stories, count numbers, and discover the world. This helps them grow up to be amazing doctors, teachers, scientists, or even presidents! Yvonne worked hard in school, and she never gave up. She knew that every book she read, every math problem she solved, and every new word she learned was making her smarter and stronger. She wanted to use her knowledge to help others someday.

A Special Meeting

As Yvonne grew up, she met a kind man named Julius Maada Bio. He was also a very thoughtful person who cared a lot about his country. They became very good friends, and later, they fell in love! It was a very special love story. Julius had a big dream too. He wanted to be the President of Sierra Leone to make his country a wonderful place for everyone. And guess what? He worked very hard, and he became the President! This meant that Yvonne became the First Lady. Did you know? A President is like the captain of a very big ship – a country! They help make rules and plans to keep everyone safe and happy.

What Does a First Lady Do?

Being a First Lady is a very important job! It means you get to help the President and the whole country. Yvonne uses her voice and her ideas to help people, especially children and women. She wants every child in Sierra Leone to have a happy and healthy life, just like she dreamed of having when she was little. She travels all over Sierra Leone, meeting families and listening to their stories. She wants to understand what they need so she can help make things better. She works on many special projects to improve lives.

Helping Children Shine Bright!

One of the biggest things Yvonne, the First Lady, cares about is making sure all children go to school. She knows how important learning is because it changed her life! She wants every boy and girl to have books, teachers, and a safe place to learn. She also works hard to make sure children are healthy. This means helping them get good food to eat and medicine when they are sick. Healthy children can learn and play much better! Yvonne believes that every child deserves to be strong and full of energy. Did you know? There are over 50 countries in Africa! Each one has its own unique stories, cultures, and amazing people. Yvonne also helps to make sure children are safe. She wants them to grow up in homes and communities where they feel loved and protected. She uses her strong voice to speak up for children who might need extra help and care. She is like a super protector for kids!

Your Dreams Matter Too!

Yvonne's story teaches us a very important lesson: no matter how tricky things might be when you are small, you can always dream big. You can work hard, learn a lot, and grow up to do amazing things that help many, many people. Just like Yvonne, you can be brave. You can love to learn. And you can grow up to make a wonderful difference in your own community, your country, and even the whole world! What will your big dream be? So, the next time you hear about Sierra Leone, remember First Lady Yvonne. Remember her brave heart, her love for school, and how she helps children shine bright! She is a true inspiration from our fantastic African continent. Keep dreaming, little ones!
summary: Meet Yvonne, the kind First Lady of Sierra Leone, who dreamed big, went to school, and now helps all the children in her country have a better life.
whyMatters: Yvonne's story teaches us that even if life is hard, you can be brave, learn a lot, and grow up to do amazing things to help others and make a big difference.

originalTitle: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?
title: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?
content: An outbreak in DR Congo has been declared a health emergency, with almost 250 suspected cases reported.

originalTitle: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship docks in the Netherlands
title: A Big Boat Arrives in a Faraway Land!
content:
Hello, My Little African News Superstars!

Hi everyone! It’s your friend here, bringing you exciting news from all over the world! Today, we have a very special story about a super big boat that travelled a long, long way. Imagine a boat so big it’s like a floating hotel, or even a small village on the water! Isn't that amazing?

The MV Hondius is Here!

Our story begins with a very big boat named the MV Hondius. Can you say that? MV Hondius! It’s a special kind of boat that helps people explore cold places, like where there are lots of ice and polar bears! It's like a floating adventure house. This big boat just finished a long journey across the big, big ocean.

Where Did It Go?

The MV Hondius sailed all the way to a country called the Netherlands. The Netherlands is a very interesting place far away from our beautiful Africa. It’s in a part of the world called Europe. The boat arrived in a big city in the Netherlands called Rotterdam. Rotterdam has a super busy place where all the big boats go. We call this place a 'port'.

What is a Port?

Think of a port like a special parking spot for very, very big boats. It's where these huge ships can stop after their long journeys. At the port, people can get off the boat, and new people can get on. It’s also where boats get more food, water, and fuel to help them keep sailing. Rotterdam’s port is one of the biggest in the whole wide world! Many ships from all over the globe come here. It's like a big welcoming mat for boats.

Who Was On Board the Big Boat?

Now, let's talk about the people on the MV Hondius. There were 25 super helpful grown-ups working on the boat. We call them 'crew members'. The crew members are like the team that makes sure everything on the boat runs smoothly. Some of them drive the boat, some cook delicious food, some keep the rooms clean, and some help everyone have a good time. They are very important because they help the boat travel safely and comfortably. And guess what? There were also two very special grown-ups on board. They were doctors and nurses! We call them 'medical personnel'. Their job is super important because they help people feel better if they get a little sick or hurt. Just like when your mommy or daddy takes you to the clinic if you don't feel well, these doctors and nurses take care of everyone on the big boat.

Understanding Hantavirus (A Tiny Germ)

Now, sometimes, tiny, tiny germs can make people feel unwell. These germs are so small, you can't even see them without a special magnifying glass! On the MV Hondius, some people might have caught a tiny germ called 'Hantavirus'. Don't worry, it's not a scary monster! It's just a type of germ that can make people feel a bit sick, like having a cold or a little fever. When people get this germ, they need to rest and get help from doctors. **Did you know?** Just like we wash our hands to get rid of germs after playing, doctors and nurses know exactly what to do to help people who have caught a germ like Hantavirus. They are very clever and kind!

Safety First!

When the MV Hondius arrived at the port in the Netherlands, the grown-ups made sure that everyone on the boat was safe. They were extra careful because of the Hantavirus germ. The doctors and nurses who were already on the boat, and new doctors waiting at the port, immediately started checking on everyone. They wanted to make sure that anyone who felt unwell could get the help they needed right away. It's like when you go to school, your teachers make sure everyone is safe and healthy. **Did you know?** Always washing your hands with soap and water, especially before you eat, is one of the best ways to keep tiny germs away and stay healthy!

Why Do Big Boats Travel So Far?

Boats like the MV Hondius travel across oceans to take people on amazing adventures. They visit different countries and see incredible things, like icy lands or warm sunny beaches. Sometimes, big boats also carry important things, like food, toys, clothes, and even cars, from one country to another. This helps people all over the world get the things they need and want. **Did you know?** Even though the Netherlands is very far from Africa, boats and aeroplanes connect us all! People from Africa sometimes travel to the Netherlands, and people from the Netherlands sometimes visit Africa!

Helping Each Other Stay Healthy

This story shows us how important it is for everyone to work together, especially when someone feels unwell. The crew members, the medical personnel, and the people at the port all worked as a team to make sure everyone on the MV Hondius was looked after. We can all learn from this: if we help each other and listen to our grown-ups about staying healthy, we can face any tiny germ or problem together! Remember to always keep yourself clean, eat your healthy food, and play outside! And don't forget to listen to your grown-ups, because they always want what’s best for you!

Bye for Now!

That's all for today's news from your friend! Stay curious, stay happy, and stay healthy until next time!
summary: A large exploration boat called the MV Hondius arrived in the Netherlands with 25 crew members and 2 medical staff, and some people on board might have caught a tiny germ called Hantavirus, so doctors are making sure everyone is safe and healthy.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows how doctors and helpers keep everyone safe and healthy, even on big boats that travel far, reminding us to always take care of ourselves and each other.

originalTitle: How war affects civilians for generations
title: How war affects civilians for generations
content: War can have a hidden legacy that no one talks about.

originalTitle: Video: US calls on G7 to impose US-led sanctions on Iran
title: Friends and Rules: A Big Story from Far Away!
content:
Hello, Friends! A Big Story!

Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa here, bringing you a big story from far, far away. Imagine your classroom, right? Sometimes, friends in a classroom might have a little disagreement. Maybe someone isn't sharing their colourful crayons, or someone said something that wasn't very kind. What do you do? You talk about it, don't you? And maybe you try to find a way to make things better so everyone can play happily again and be good friends. Well, something similar is happening in the big, big world of countries. There are many countries, just like there are many children in a school. And sometimes, countries have big conversations about how to make sure everyone is safe and happy across the world. This is a story about one of those important conversations.

Countries Are Like Big Families

Let's talk about countries. A country is like a giant home for many, many people. It has its own rules, its own leaders, and its own special things. Two of these countries, very far from us here in Africa, are the United States (we can call it the US for short) and another country called Iran. Both the US and Iran are big places with lots of interesting history and people. Sometimes, countries don't agree on everything, just like friends sometimes don't agree on which game to play. When big countries want to talk to another country about something important, and it's not working, they might try a special way to get their attention. Did you know? The United States is a very, very big country! It's so big, you could fit many, many countries from Africa inside it! And Iran is also a very old country with lots of amazing stories from a long, long time ago.

What Are Sanctions? (It's Like a Time-Out)

This special way to get attention is called 'sanctions'. It's a tricky word, but let's make it simple. Think of it like this: Imagine you and your friends have a special game. If one friend keeps breaking the rules, maybe you say, 'We won't play with your favourite toy or share our yummy snacks with you until we all agree to follow the rules and be kind.' It's not about being mean; it's about helping everyone play fair and be safe. Sanctions are like that, but for whole countries. It means some countries decide they won't buy certain things from Iran, or sell some things to Iran, for a little while. This is not because they want to fight. No, no! It's because they want Iran to think very hard about why this is happening and maybe change some things that other countries are worried about. The idea is that if Iran finds it harder to trade and do business with many countries, it will see how important it is to listen to the concerns of others. It's a way to try and solve big problems without fighting, to make the world a safer and more peaceful place for everyone. It’s like giving a country a 'time-out' to think about things. Did you know? Countries trade lots of things! They sell food, clothes, machines, and many other things to each other. When countries put 'sanctions' on, it means they stop some of this trading.

Who is Talking? The Money Boss!

So, who is talking about these sanctions? In the United States, there's a very important person called the US Treasury Secretary. This person is like the chief money manager for the whole country! They look after all the money and how the US does business with other countries. They have a really big job to make sure the country's money is used wisely. This important money manager from the US is asking other countries, like a big group of friends, to join them in this special way of talking. They want many countries to agree to use sanctions. When many countries agree, it makes the message much, much stronger, like many voices singing together. Did you know? A Treasury Secretary is a bit like the head of a big bank for a whole country! They help decide how money is spent and saved.

The G7 Club: Countries Talking Together

There's a special club of some of the biggest and most powerful countries in the world called the G7. G7 stands for 'Group of Seven'. These countries are Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom, and the United States. They are like the leaders of a big classroom or a big village meeting. These G7 countries talk a lot about important things that affect everyone, especially money and how countries get along peacefully. The US is asking the G7, and other countries too, to say, 'Yes, we agree! We will also take part in these 'sanctions' to encourage Iran to do things differently.' It’s like many friends agreeing on a rule for the playground. When many friends agree, it makes the message even stronger and easier to understand. Did you know? The G7 leaders meet every single year to talk about big world problems and how to solve them. They try to work together to make the world a better place!

Why This Is Important for Everyone (Even Us in Africa!)

Now, you might be thinking, 'The US and Iran are so far away from us in Africa! Why does this story matter to me?' Well, my wonderful friends, remember we are all part of one big, beautiful world! Just like everyone in your class is connected, all countries are connected too. When countries talk about peace and how to make sure everyone is safe, it's good news for everyone, everywhere. We all want a world where countries are good neighbours and solve problems by talking, not by fighting or being unkind. When big countries try to find peaceful ways to solve problems, it helps make the whole world a safer, happier place for all of us, including us here in Africa. Peace is like sunshine for everyone. When there is peace, everyone can learn, grow, and play without worry. Children can go to school, families can be together, and everyone can dream big dreams. So, when countries work together to encourage peace and solve problems, it's a big deal for all of us! It means a brighter future for children everywhere. Did you know? Africa has 54 different countries! That means we have lots and lots of neighbours and friends right here on our amazing continent!

Let's Hope for Peace!

So, the US is asking its friends to help send a strong message to Iran, hoping that everyone can agree on rules that help keep the world safe and peaceful. It's all about trying to solve problems by talking and working together, so that all countries can be good neighbours. Let’s hope for a world full of peace and friendship!
summary: The United States is asking a group of big countries called the G7, and others, to join them in a special way called 'sanctions' to encourage the country of Iran to change some things and help make the world a safer place.
whyMatters: This matters because when big countries work together to solve problems by talking instead of fighting, it helps make the whole world, including us in Africa, a safer and more peaceful place for everyone to live and play.

originalTitle: Trump warns 'clock is ticking' for Iran as peace progress stalls
title: Countries Talking: Let's Make Peace!
content:
Hello, Friends from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter here, bringing you big news from far away. We're going to talk about something really important that grown-ups in different countries are doing. It's all about trying to make the world a peaceful place for everyone. Imagine you have a puzzle, and some pieces don't fit quite right. You need to talk and work together to make them fit. That's a bit like what countries do!

What's This Big News About?

Today, we are looking at news about two big countries. One is called America, and the other is called Iran. These countries have had some disagreements, just like friends sometimes do. Their leaders are trying very hard to find ways to be peaceful and solve their problems. It’s like when you and your friend talk about who gets to play with a toy first, so you can both be happy.

Meet Mr. Trump and America!

Mr. Trump used to be a very important leader. He was the President of America. A President is like the head of a big family, but for a whole country! America is a huge country. It is very, very far away from us here in Africa, across a big ocean. It has many cities and lots of people. Did you know? The President of America lives and works in a special house called the White House! It is white, just like its name!

Meet Iran!

Iran is another country. It also has its own people and its own important leaders. Iran is also very far away from us, but in a different direction from America. People in Iran have different cultures and ways of life, just like different countries in Africa have their own special ways. They have their own stories and traditions. Did you know? Iran is famous for its beautiful, handmade carpets! They are often very colourful and have amazing patterns that take a long time to make.

"Clock is Ticking!" What Does That Mean?

Imagine you are playing a game, and your grown-up says, "Hurry up! The clock is ticking!" This means you don't have much time left. You need to make a decision or finish something quickly. Mr. Trump, the leader from America, used these words to tell Iran that they need to make decisions faster about peace talks. He wanted them to hurry up. It means they need to talk and agree on things before time runs out completely. He felt the talks were taking too long.

Talking for Peace, But It's Slow

It is super important for countries to talk to each other when they have problems. This way, they can find solutions without fighting. This special way of talking is called 'diplomacy'. But sometimes, talking about big problems can be very, very slow. It can be like watching a snail slowly move along a leaf. When talks are slow and not moving forward, we say they are "stalling." The news said that the peace talks were stalling. This means they weren't moving forward quickly. It's like when your car gets stuck in the mud and can't go anywhere. Did you know? Talking is always the best way to solve problems, even if it takes a long time! It's much better than fighting.

What is a 'Proposal'?

America made a 'proposal' to Iran. What is a proposal? It's like an idea or a plan that one person or country offers to another. It's a suggestion to solve a problem. Think of it like this: if you want to play a new game, you might tell your friends your idea. "Let's play hide-and-seek!" That's your proposal! America sent its idea to Iran, hoping they could agree on it. Iran thought about it carefully and has now sent its answer back to America. It's like sending a letter back and forth!

Are They Still Talking? Yes!

This is good news! The news item said that America and Iran are still 'exchanging' ideas. This means they are still sending messages and talking to each other. They haven't stopped! They haven't stopped talking, which is a great sign! It shows they still want to try and work things out peacefully. It's like when you and your friends keep chatting, even if you don't agree on everything yet. These important talks often happen in big, important cities. Sometimes, they talk in Washington D.C., which is a very big and important city in America. Did you know? Washington D.C. is the capital city of the USA. A capital city is where a country's main government buildings are, like the President's house!

Why is Peace So Important?

Peace means no fighting. It means everyone can live happily and safely in their homes and countries. When there is peace, children can go to school every day without worries. Families can grow their food, play outside, and feel safe. Peace helps everyone to live a good life and dream big dreams. We here in Africa know how wonderful peace is, and we wish for it for all countries, always. When countries are peaceful, they can help each other, share ideas, and make the world a better place for all children. Peace makes everyone happy and healthy. Did you know? Many people all over the world work hard to help countries find peace, so that everyone can be safe and happy, no matter where they live.

Our Hope for a Peaceful World!

So, even though these talks might be slow, it's really good that America and Iran are still trying to talk to each other. Talking is the first step to solving problems. Remember, just like you talk to your family and friends to solve disagreements, countries need to talk too. It makes everything better. Let's all hope that America and Iran keep talking and find a way to have peace soon. This way, children everywhere can grow up in a safe world. Thank you for listening to the news today, my young friends! Keep being kind, keep talking, and stay curious!
summary: Leaders from America and Iran are trying to talk about making peace, but it's taking a long time, and America wants them to hurry up.
whyMatters: When countries talk instead of fight, it helps keep everyone safe and happy, so children can play and go to school without worry.

originalTitle: Bodies of missing Italian divers found in Maldives
title: A Sad Story from Far Away: Ocean Explorers Found
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends in Africa! It’s your news friend here, ready to share a story with you. Sometimes, news can be a little sad, but it’s important to understand what happens in our big world. Today, we have some news from a place very, very far away from us. It’s a beautiful place called the Maldives. Can you say that? Mal-dives. It's full of blue oceans and pretty islands.

What Happened to the Ocean Explorers?

There were five brave people. They were from a country called Italy. Italy is also far away, across the big blue ocean. These five people loved to go deep into the ocean. They were called divers. Divers are like explorers who swim under the water. Last week, these five divers went on an adventure. They went diving in the beautiful waters of the Maldives. But then, something sad happened. They went missing. This means no one could find them for a while. Imagine playing hide-and-seek, but for a very long time, and you can't find your friends. Everyone was very worried. Their families were sad. Many people helped to look for them.

What is Diving?

Diving is when people wear special suits and tanks on their backs. These tanks hold air, so they can breathe underwater! They also wear big flippers on their feet, like fish tails, to help them swim fast. Divers love to see the colourful fish, amazing coral, and all the secret things hiding in the deep blue sea. Did you know? Some divers can go very, very deep. It's like visiting a whole other world down there! But it's also important to be very careful and always dive with friends.

Where are the Maldives?

The Maldives is a country made of many, many tiny islands. Imagine lots of little dots floating in the ocean! The water there is so clear, you can see the fish swimming from above. People from all over the world love to visit the Maldives because it's so beautiful. Did you know? The Maldives is famous for its amazing coral reefs. Coral reefs are like underwater cities built by tiny sea creatures. Lots of colourful fish live there, like Nemo!

Looking for Our Friends

When the divers went missing, many people started to help. They used special boats and even planes to look for them. Everyone hoped they would be found safe and sound. It was a very hard time for their families and friends in Italy. Thinking about someone you love going missing is a very sad feeling. Everyone wanted to help bring them home. People prayed and hoped for good news.

The News They Were Found

Now, we have some news to share. Officials, who are like grown-up helpers who know a lot about news, told a news channel called the BBC that all five divers have now been found. This is still very sad news because it means their adventure in the ocean has ended. But finding them brings some comfort to their families. It helps them to know what happened. It’s like when you lose a favourite toy. Even if it’s broken, finding it helps you to say goodbye properly. Their families now know where their loved ones are.

Saying Goodbye

It's very sad when people you care about are not with us anymore. These divers loved the ocean, and they were doing something they loved. We send our thoughts to their families in Italy. Did you know? The ocean is huge! It covers most of our planet. There are so many amazing creatures and places to explore in it. That's why divers love it so much.

Always Be Safe

This story reminds us that even when we are doing something fun, like swimming or exploring, it’s always important to be safe. Listen to your parents and teachers. Don't go somewhere you shouldn't, and always tell someone where you are going. Even though this happened far away, it teaches us about bravery, sadness, and the importance of helping each other when someone is in trouble. We must always look out for one another. It’s a big world, my friends, full of amazing things to see and do. But always remember to be careful and stay safe. Thank you for listening to this story today. We’ll be back with more news soon!
summary: Officials have found the bodies of five Italian divers who went missing last week while exploring the beautiful ocean in the Maldives, bringing sad but comforting news to their families.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us about a brave but sad adventure far away, reminding us to always be safe when exploring and to care for people around the world.

originalTitle: At least six Americans exposed to Ebola during DR Congo outbreak
title: Our Heroes in Africa: Learning About Ebola and Staying Healthy!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! I'm your friendly news reporter, here to share important stories from our amazing continent, Africa. Today, we're going to talk about something very important: staying healthy and safe. Sometimes, we hear about tiny, tiny germs that can make people sick. One of these germs is called Ebola. But don't worry! We have many smart doctors and kind helpers working very hard to keep everyone safe. Let's learn together about what Ebola is and how we can all be super healthy.

What is Ebola, and Why Are We Talking About It?

Ebola is a germ. It's a tiny, tiny thing that can live in our world. Germs are so small, you can't even see them with your eyes! You need a special magnifying glass, called a microscope, to see them. Ebola germs can make people sick. It's not like catching a cold or a little cough. It's a different kind of sickness. It can make people feel very unwell. **Did you know?** There are many kinds of germs all around us! Some are good, but some can make us sick. Washing our hands helps get rid of the bad ones. Ebola is very rare. This means it doesn't happen often, and it's not everywhere. It usually only happens in a few special places, for a short time. People usually get Ebola if they touch someone who is very, very sick with it. This is why doctors and nurses are extra careful. They wear special clothes to protect themselves when they help sick people. They are like superheroes fighting invisible enemies!

A Story from Our Neighbour: DR Congo

Our continent, Africa, is very, very big. It has many, many countries. Each country is special and beautiful. One of these countries is called the Democratic Republic of Congo. It's sometimes called DR Congo for short. It's a neighbour to many other African countries. Sometimes, in a very small part of DR Congo, a few people have gotten sick with Ebola. When a few people get sick in one place, we call it an "outbreak." **Did you know?** Africa is the second-largest continent in the whole world! It has 54 different countries, each with its own special languages and cultures. Most of Africa is completely safe, happy, and healthy! These outbreaks are usually in very small, specific areas. Our doctors and leaders work very hard to keep these germs from spreading to other places. They want everyone to be safe.

Special Helpers from Around the World

When people get sick, many kind people rush to help them. These helpers are often doctors, nurses, and scientists. They are very brave. Some helpers come from different countries to give a hand. They travel a long way to make sure people get better. Right now, some helpers from a country called America are in DR Congo. They are smart doctors and health workers. They are there to take care of the sick and stop the germs. **Did you know?** Health workers are heroes! They spend their lives learning how to help others feel better and stay safe. We should always thank our doctors and nurses for their amazing work! These helpers are very dedicated. This means they care a lot about their job. They want to make sure everyone is healthy. We are so lucky to have such wonderful people.

Being Super Careful: What Happened?

We heard that six helpers from America were very close to someone who had Ebola. When you are very close to a sick person, we say you were "exposed" to the germ. Being exposed does NOT mean you are sick. It just means you were near the germ. It's like standing near someone who is sneezing – you *might* catch a cold, but you might not! One of these six American helpers felt a little bit unwell. Feeling unwell means feeling a bit tired, or having a small tummy ache, or not feeling quite right. It does not mean they definitely have Ebola. Many things can make you feel unwell, like just being tired from working hard! Even missing your family can make you feel a bit sad or unwell. But because they were exposed, they are being extra, extra careful. They are resting and watching themselves very closely. Other doctors are checking on them all the time. This is very smart! If you are ever around someone sick, it's good to be extra careful too. It helps keep everyone safe. It's a way of being responsible.

Super Powers for Staying Healthy!

We all have super powers to fight germs! The very best super power is washing our hands. It's a simple thing that makes a big difference! **Wash your hands often with soap and water!** Make sure you rub them together for a long time. You can sing a little song while you wash, like "Happy Birthday" twice. This usually takes about 20 seconds. This washes all the tiny, tiny germs away, including Ebola germs if they were there. It's like putting on a shield against invisible bad guys! **Did you know?** Soap and water are like magic for cleaning your hands. They break down the germs and wash them away. Always wash before you eat and after you use the toilet. Try not to touch your face – your eyes, nose, or mouth – especially if your hands are not clean. Germs love to sneak in through these places. If someone is very sick, it's best to keep a little distance. This is not because you are mean, but to help everyone stay safe. Always tell a grown-up if you feel unwell or if you see someone who is very sick. Your parents, teachers, and guardians are there to help you!

Our Heroes are Working Hard!

Doctors and scientists are like detectives for germs. They work super hard to understand how germs work. They want to find the best ways to stop them. When doctors are helping people with Ebola, they wear special suits. These suits look a bit like astronaut suits! They cover their whole body. These suits keep them safe from the germs, so they don't get sick while helping others. They are very brave. Scientists also invent special medicines called "vaccines." A vaccine is like a special training for your body. It teaches your body how to fight germs even before you meet them! It's like giving your body a map to find the bad guys. **Did you know?** Scientists have found a special vaccine for Ebola! This vaccine is helping many people stay safe in DR Congo. It's wonderful news because it means we are getting better and better at fighting these germs. Science is amazing!

Don't Be Scared, Be Smart!

It's important to learn about things like Ebola, but it's also very important not to be scared. Remember, Ebola is very, very rare. It only happens in small pockets of our big continent. Most people in Africa, and all over the world, are healthy and happy every day. Our brave doctors, nurses, and scientists are doing an amazing job. They are working day and night to stop the germs and help everyone feel better. By washing our hands, telling grown-ups if we feel unwell, and listening to our leaders, we can all help too. Every small action helps keep our communities safe and strong. Remember, Africa is a continent full of joy, music, beautiful nature, and wonderful people. We are strong, and we look out for each other. So, stay curious, stay safe, and keep those hands clean! See you next time, my friends!
summary: Brave doctors and helpers in DR Congo are working hard to stop a rare germ called Ebola, and we can all stay safe by washing our hands often.
whyMatters: Learning about Ebola helps us understand how doctors keep us safe and reminds us to always wash our hands to fight tiny germs and stay healthy.

originalTitle: Selfish Salah should be dropped from Anfield finale - Rooney
title: Football Fun: Big News About Our Star, Mohamed Salah!
content:
Hello, Super Friends from Across Africa!

Guess what? Your favorite kid reporter is back with some exciting news from the world of football! We love watching our heroes score amazing goals and celebrate, don't we? Today, we're going to talk about a very special football star, Mohamed Salah, who comes from our amazing continent, Africa!

Meet Our African King: Mohamed Salah!

Mohamed Salah is one of the most famous football players in the whole world! He is from Egypt, a wonderful country in North Africa. We call him the 'Egyptian King' because he plays so brilliantly! He runs super fast and scores so many goals for his team, Liverpool FC. When Salah plays, everyone gets excited! Did you know? Mohamed Salah is known for being very kind off the field too. He helps many people in his home country, Egypt, by building schools and hospitals! He makes us all very proud.

What is Football (Soccer) All About?

Football is a super fun game where two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's net to score points, called goals! It's played on a big green field with lots of running, passing, and cheering. It's like playing with your friends in the park, but on a much bigger stage! Everyone on the team has a special job to do.

Meet Another Football Hero: Wayne Rooney!

Now, let's talk about another famous football star named Wayne Rooney. He used to play for another very big team called Manchester United, and he also scored lots and lots of goals! Wayne Rooney is from a country called England, which is very far away from Africa. He knows a lot about playing football and being part of a team. Did you know? Wayne Rooney started playing football when he was a little boy, just like many of you might be doing now! He worked very hard to become one of the best players ever.

A Little Chat About Teamwork!

Imagine you are playing a game with your friends. Maybe you want to build a tall sandcastle, but your friend wants to dig a big hole. Sometimes, friends have different ideas, right? In football, it's the same! All the players on a team need to work together like a big family to win the game. They need to pass the ball to each other and help each other. Teamwork means thinking about what is best for everyone, not just one person. It's like when you share your toys with your friends so everyone can have fun! When everyone helps, amazing things can happen.

What Happened with Salah and His Team?

Recently, our superstar Mohamed Salah said some things about his team, Liverpool. Sometimes, even the best players might feel a little bit sad or have different ideas about how things should be done in the team. It's like when you tell your teacher that you wish you could have more playtime, or do something a different way! Salah just wanted what he thought was best for his team, but maybe he showed his feelings in a way that some people didn't quite agree with. It happens sometimes, even with grown-ups!

What Did Wayne Rooney Think?

Wayne Rooney, our other football hero, heard what Salah said. Rooney thought that maybe Salah was thinking a little bit too much about his own feelings right then. He felt that everyone on the team, especially for the very last game of the season, needs to think about the team first. Rooney thought that maybe Salah should take a little break from playing that last game, just so everyone can remember to work together really, really well. Did you know? The 'season' in football is like a whole school year, where teams play many games. The 'finale' means the very last game!

Why is Thinking About the Team Super Important?

Think about building that sandcastle again. If one friend only wants to play with their own special bucket and doesn't help with the big castle, it might not get finished, or it won't be as tall and strong! In football, if players only think about themselves and don't pass the ball or help their teammates, the team might not score goals or win the game. Working together means trusting your friends and helping them, even when you have different ideas. It's about being a strong unit!

Learning From Our Football Friends

This story reminds us that it's okay for people to have different ideas. But it's super important to be kind, talk things out, and always try to work together as a team. Even big football stars like Mohamed Salah and Wayne Rooney sometimes need to remember how important friendship and teamwork are! Football teaches us many good lessons, like playing fair, being a good sport, and helping our friends. These lessons are important not just on the field, but also in school and at home with our families.

The Last Big Game!

Liverpool, Mohamed Salah's team, has one more very important game to play. It's like the very last day of school before a long holiday – everyone wants to finish strong and have fun! We hope all the players remember to play fair, help each other, and do their very best. Let's all cheer for teamwork and good sportsmanship! Remember, whether you're playing football, building a sandcastle, or doing schoolwork, working together makes everything better! Go team! Thank you for reading, my super friends!
summary: Famous football star Mohamed Salah had some different ideas about his team, and fellow player Wayne Rooney thinks he should focus more on teamwork for the last big game.
whyMatters: This story reminds us how important it is for everyone, even super famous players, to work together like good friends in a team, and that talking things out kindly is always best.

originalTitle: BBC Sport announces World Cup coverage plans
title: Get Ready! We Can Watch ALL World Cup Games!
content:
Hello, amazing football fans of Africa! Guess what? I have some super exciting news for you today!

A Super Big Football Party is Coming!

Imagine a giant party where teams from all over the world come to play football. That's what the FIFA World Cup is! It's the biggest football party on Earth! Every four years, countries send their best players to compete. Everyone watches and cheers for their favourite teams. It's so much fun! Here in Africa, we love football so much! We always hope our brave African teams will play their very best and make us proud. They are real heroes! Did you know? FIFA stands for Fédération Internationale de Football Association. It's the big boss of football all over the world!

Watching All The Games! Yay!

Now, for the super exciting news! A special company called BBC Sport wants to make sure we can all watch the next World Cup. This will happen in the year 2026. When we say 'watch coverage,' it means they will show all the games on TV. Maybe even on the radio too, so we can listen! How cool is that? BBC Sport is like a friend who helps us see all the amazing football action. They will show every single game, so we don't miss any goals or awesome saves! Did you know? People started watching football on TV a very long time ago, even before your parents were born! It was black and white then!

The Amazing 2026 World Cup!

The World Cup in 2026 will be extra special. It will be the biggest World Cup ever! More teams will play, and there will be many, many more games. Imagine this: usually, there are 64 games. But in 2026, there will be 104 games! That's so many matches to watch and enjoy! More football fun for everyone! More games mean more chances to see incredible goals, fantastic dribbles, and happy celebrations. Every game is a chance for a new hero to shine. Did you know? The 2026 World Cup will have 48 teams competing, which is more than ever before! More teams mean more countries get to try and win!

Where Will The Magic Happen?

So, where will all these exciting games be played? Not just in one country, but in three big countries! They are the United States of America, Canada, and Mexico. These countries are very far away from us in Africa. They are across the big ocean. But don't worry, BBC Sport will bring the games right into our homes! Imagine players flying to these different cities to play their matches. It's a huge journey for the teams, but they do it all for the love of the game and their country! Did you know? The United States, Canada, and Mexico are all in a part of the world called North America. It's a very big place!

Our African Heroes!

We are all going to be super excited to see which African teams will go to the World Cup in 2026. Our teams work very hard and train every day. We will cheer for them with all our hearts! When an African team plays, it feels like our whole continent is playing. We want them to score many goals! It's a wonderful feeling to see our flags waving and our songs being sung on the world stage. Our players are strong and fast, and they have amazing skills. Did you know? Many famous football players from Africa play in big leagues around the world, and then they come back to play for their home countries!

Get Ready For Fun!

So, my dear friends, get ready for a fantastic football time in 2026! It's a chance to learn about other countries, see amazing sportsmanship, and cheer very loud. Even though it's still a little while away, it's good to know that we won't miss any of the action. We can watch every kick, every save, and every winning moment. Football brings people together. We can watch with our families and friends, share snacks, and celebrate every goal. It's a happy time for everyone! Did you know? The World Cup trophy is called the FIFA World Cup Trophy, and it's made of real gold! Only the winning team gets to lift it!
summary: Get ready, Africa! BBC Sport will show us all 104 exciting games of the super-sized 2026 FIFA World Cup from the United States, Canada, and Mexico!
whyMatters: This matters because we all love football, and now we know we won't miss any of the big games from the World Cup in 2026!

originalTitle: 'I was screaming' - footballer stabbed seven times recalls mass knife attack
title: Jonathan the Footballer: A Story of Courage and Helping Hands!
content:
Hello, super kids of Africa! Are you ready for a story about a very brave friend? His name is Jonathan Gjoshe. He loves to play football, just like many of you! Jonathan is a kind and strong man. One day, he was on a train, and something not so good happened. But guess what? He showed us all how to be super brave!

A Journey on the Train

Imagine you are going on a fun trip. You sit on a big, fast train. That's what Jonathan was doing! He was travelling in a faraway country called England. The train was taking him from one town to another. Maybe he was thinking about his next football game, or perhaps he was just looking out the window. Trains are great because they can take many people at once! *Did you know?* Trains are like long, friendly snakes that carry people and things across lands! They run on special tracks made of metal.

A Scary Moment

Then, something very unexpected happened. A person on the train started to act very badly. This person had a sharp knife, which is a dangerous thing. This person hurt many people on the train. Jonathan was one of them. He got hurt many times by the sharp knife. It was a very scary moment for everyone there. Imagine feeling scared and wanting to be safe. That's how Jonathan and many others felt. There were 11 people who got hurt quite a lot on that day. *Simple explanation:* A knife is a sharp tool we use to cut food, like vegetables or bread. But it can be very dangerous if someone uses it to hurt people. It's important to remember that knives are not toys and should always be handled by grown-ups carefully.

Jonathan's Brave Heart

When Jonathan was hurt, he was very, very scared. He even shouted loudly because of the pain and fear. But even when he was scared, Jonathan was brave. He tried to protect himself and others on the train. Being brave doesn't mean you are not scared. It means you are scared, but you still try to be strong and get through it. Jonathan is a true hero for facing such a difficult moment. *Did you know?* Heroes are not just in storybooks! People like Jonathan, who show courage when things are tough and help others, are real-life heroes we can learn from.

Help Arrives Like Superheroes!

After the bad person was stopped, many helpers came very quickly! These helpers were like superheroes. Doctors and nurses came to help everyone who was hurt, including Jonathan. They worked very fast and were very kind. They put bandages on the wounds and gave medicine to help with the pain. When bad things happen, it's wonderful to know there are always good people ready to help. *Did you know?* Emergency helpers like doctors, nurses, police officers, and firefighters train very hard. They learn how to help people quickly and safely when something bad happens, like an accident or an emergency.

Getting Better, Bit by Bit

Jonathan had to stay in the hospital for some time. The doctors and nurses took very good care of him. They made sure he was comfortable and healing well. His family and friends visited him every day. They brought him smiles and encouraging words. Getting better takes time, like a little plant growing back strong after a storm. Each day, Jonathan felt a little stronger and a little better. He worked hard to heal his body and his mind.

The Power of Kindness and Support

When Jonathan was healing, so many people showed him kindness. They sent cards to cheer him up, they cooked food for his family, and they just listened to him. This support helped Jonathan's heart feel better, not just his body. When we are kind to others, it makes them feel loved and strong. It helps them to smile even when they are sad or worried. Kindness is a superpower we all have inside us! It makes the world a better place. *Simple explanation:* Support means giving help and care to someone who needs it. It can be a hug, a kind word, or just being there for them to show you care.

Lessons from Jonathan's Story

Jonathan's story teaches us some very important things: 1. **Be Brave:** Even when things are scary, we can find courage inside us to face challenges. Remember that it's okay to be scared, but still try to be strong. 2. **Help Others:** If you see someone in trouble, always try to get help from a trusted grown-up, like your teacher, parent, or a police officer. 3. **Be Kind:** When someone is hurt or sad, a little kindness can make a big difference. Your warm heart can help someone feel better. 4. **Good People Help:** Even if bad things happen, there are always good people who will come to help and make things right again. Jonathan is now recovering well. He hopes to play football again soon! His story reminds us to cherish safety and to always be ready to help one another. *Did you know?* Many people who go through tough times often become even stronger and kinder. They learn to appreciate life and all the good things even more!

A Hopeful Message

So, my dear friends, remember Jonathan Gjoshe. He is a brave footballer who went through a tough time but is getting better because of his courage and the help of many kind people. Let's all try to be brave, kind, and helpful, just like the heroes in this story!
summary: Jonathan Gjoshe, a brave footballer, was hurt during a scary incident on a train but is recovering thanks to his courage and the kindness of many helpers.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us to be brave when things are scary, to always help others, and to remember that there are many kind people who will come to our rescue.
